# DNS Records — Google Workspace Email Setup

These DNS records prevent your emails from landing in spam and prove to receiving mail servers that you own the domain.

---

## 1. SPF Record (add to your domain's DNS)

| Host | Type | Value |
|------|------|-------|
| `@` | TXT | `v=spf1 include:_spf.google.com ~all` |

> If an SPF record already exists on `@`, **merge** — never add a second TXT record.
> Example: `v=spf1 include:_spf.google.com include:sendgrid.net ~all`

---

## 2. DKIM Record (generated by Google Workspace)

DKIM is a cryptographic signature Google adds to outgoing mail. You must generate the key in the Admin Console:

1. Sign in to [Google Workspace Admin Console](https://admin.google.com)
2. Go to **Apps → Google Workspace → Gmail → Authenticate email**
3. Select your domain → **Generate new record**
4. Key length: choose **2048-bit** (recommended)
5. Add the record Google shows you to your DNS:

| Host | Type | Value |
|------|------|-------|
| `google._domainkey` | TXT | `v=DKIM1; k=rsa; p=<YOUR_PUBLIC_KEY>` |

6. Back in the Admin Console, click **Start authentication** once the record has propagated (may take up to 48 hours)

---

## 3. DMARC Record

DMARC tells receiving servers what to do with mail that fails SPF/DKIM.

| Host | Type | Value |
|------|------|-------|
| `_dmarc` | TXT | `v=DMARC1; p=none; rua=mailto:postmaster@yourdomain.com; fo=1` |

**Recommended rollout — change `p=` in stages:**

| Stage | Policy | When |
|-------|--------|------|
| 1 | `p=none` | First 1–2 weeks — monitor only, nothing blocked |
| 2 | `p=quarantine` | Once reports look clean — suspicious mail goes to spam |
| 3 | `p=reject` | When you're confident — suspicious mail is rejected outright |

Replace `postmaster@yourdomain.com` with a real mailbox you can monitor. Google Workspace sends aggregate reports there weekly.

---

## 4. MX Records (for receiving mail on your domain)

Only needed if you want to **receive** email at your domain via Google Workspace:

| Host | Type | Priority | Value |
|------|------|----------|-------|
| `@` | MX | 1 | `ASPMX.L.GOOGLE.COM` |
| `@` | MX | 5 | `ALT1.ASPMX.L.GOOGLE.COM` |
| `@` | MX | 5 | `ALT2.ASPMX.L.GOOGLE.COM` |
| `@` | MX | 10 | `ALT3.ASPMX.L.GOOGLE.COM` |
| `@` | MX | 10 | `ALT4.ASPMX.L.GOOGLE.COM` |

---

## App SMTP Configuration (`appsettings.json`)

Replace the placeholder values once your Google Workspace account is ready:

```json
"Smtp": {
  "Host": "smtp.gmail.com",
  "Port": 587,
  "Username": "noreply@yourdomain.com",
  "Password": "xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx",
  "SenderName": "Your Business Name",
  "SenderEmail": "noreply@yourdomain.com",
  "ReplyTo": "support@yourdomain.com",
  "UseStartTls": true
}
```

| Field | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| `Host` | Always `smtp.gmail.com` for Google Workspace |
| `Port` | `587` (STARTTLS) or `465` (set `UseStartTls: false` for SSL) |
| `Username` | Your full Google Workspace email address |
| `Password` | App Password — see below |
| `SenderName` | Display name shown in the recipient's inbox |
| `SenderEmail` | Must match `Username` or be a configured alias |
| `ReplyTo` | Where replies go — can differ from `SenderEmail` |
| `UseStartTls` | `true` for port 587, `false` for port 465 |

---

## Getting an App Password

Google Workspace requires an App Password (not your account password) for SMTP:

1. Go to your Google Account → **Security**
2. Enable **2-Step Verification** if not already on
3. Go to **Security → App passwords**
4. Select app: **Mail**, device: **Other (custom name)** → give it a name like "GmailApp SMTP"
5. Copy the 16-character password and paste it into `Password` in `appsettings.json`

---

## Keeping Credentials Out of Source Control

**Never commit real passwords to git.** Use one of these instead:

**Development — .NET User Secrets:**
```bash
dotnet user-secrets set "Smtp:Password" "your-app-password"
dotnet user-secrets set "Smtp:Username" "noreply@yourdomain.com"
```

**Production — Environment Variables:**
```
Smtp__Password=your-app-password
Smtp__Username=noreply@yourdomain.com
```

ASP.NET Core reads double-underscore (`__`) as section separator, so `Smtp__Password` maps to `Smtp:Password` in config.
